steps.text: Extramural research applications submitted to CDC are required to go through external peer review by a Federal Advisory Committee.
The CIO director must submit written justification requesting an exception from this policy in justified emergency situations.
Scientific programs conducted or funded by CDC are subject to external peer review for scientific and technical quality.
CIO directors are encouraged to implement a strategic and flexible approach to external peer review of scientific programs.
External peer review of research and non-research activities is a rigorous process that identifies strengths, weaknesses, gaps, redundancies and program effectiveness.
The first-level peer review is conducted by a panel of experts for evaluating the scientific and technical merit of research applications.
The second-level peer review involves a separate senior advisory panel to evaluate the preliminary recommendations.
Peer review of intramural research and scientific programs must address program quality, approach, direction, capability, and integrity.
Contract proposals are evaluated in a two-step process by a technical evaluation panel and by the contracting officer.
Task orders will be reviewed and selected using existing OFR guidance and procedures.
Directors of CIOs are responsible for the implementation of this policy and annual reporting of planned and completed peer review activities to the OS.
The CIO will ensure that reviews are conducted by experts external to CDC without conflict of interest.
The Director for OS is responsible for providing overall guidance to CIOs to implement and assess the utility and effectiveness of the peer review process.

preconditions.text: In justified emergency situations, an exception from external peer review can be requested.
